<findings>

[suggestion] [info] Zone "Notion Integration Adapter" (packages-rex:notion-integration) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.86, cohesion: 0.14, coupling: 0.86) — justified: Cohesion 0.14 is a zone-size artifact: with only 2 production files (notion-map.ts, notion-adapter.ts) plus their Notion schema, the ratio of internal to external imports is inherently low. The files form a tight functional unit — notion-map provides bidirectional mapping consumed exclusively by notion-adapter. Pin of integration-schemas/notion.ts reunites the Notion contract in a single zone. Physical relocation of notion-map.ts from core/ to store/ colocates it with its sole consumer.

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.14) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.86) — 8 imports target "prd-operations-core"
- The low cohesion (0.14) and high coupling (0.86) indicate this zone's two production files are tightly dependent on external modules while sharing little internal structure — consider consolidating notion-map.ts into notion-adapter.ts if mapping logic is used nowhere else.
- The zone's entry point is notion-adapter.ts (store layer) but notion-map.ts is classified as a utility — verify whether this utility is shared across adapters or private to Notion, and move it accordingly.
- This zone is fragmented from the integration-schemas zone below, which also contains a notion.ts schema; the full Notion integration surface is split across two zones, making it harder to reason about the complete adapter contract.
- Cohesion 0.14 is critically low — the two production files share almost no internal imports, suggesting they were grouped by the community algorithm on thin coupling rather than genuine structural affinity.
- Coupling 0.86 is very high — this zone draws heavily from external modules, meaning changes upstream will frequently ripple into both the adapter and its mapping utility.
- Notion integration logic is split between this zone (adapter + map) and the integration-schemas zone (notion.ts schema), which fragments the ownership of a single external service contract.
- notion-integration depends on prd-operations-core (8 imports) while integration-schemas depends on remote-integration (3 imports) — the two zones forming the Notion integration layer anchor to different internal hubs, meaning a change to either hub can independently break the Notion contract without a clear single owner to triage
- notion-integration (adapter+map) and integration-schemas (schema) anchor to different internal zones — prd-operations-core and remote-integration respectively — so the Notion integration contract has two separate internal coupling points with no shared interface zone to unify them
- notion-map.ts (utility archetype) lives in a zone whose entry point is notion-adapter.ts (store archetype) — the utility has a higher-layer host, inverting the conventional utility-below-consumer layering
- notion-integration does not appear as a source in any cross-zone import traffic, confirming it is a pure leaf consumer — but this also means its public exports are unreachable by zone-level analysis, so any API surface it exposes is consumed only through file-level imports invisible to the community graph or exclusively through CLI command wrappers
- notion-map.ts has no independent zone-level consumers — it is only reachable through notion-adapter.ts within the same zone, making it effectively private implementation of the adapter; if it has no standalone test coverage or external consumers, it should be merged into notion-adapter.ts rather than maintained as a separate file
- The zone's two test files live in different test subdirectories — tests/unit/core/notion-map.test.ts and tests/unit/store/notion-adapter.test.ts — mirroring the physical split of their source files across src/core/ and src/store/. The test directory fragmentation is a direct symptom of the source file misplacement: notion-map.ts was placed in src/core/ despite being a private utility of a store-layer adapter, and the test layout has followed that mistake.
- notion-map.ts lives in src/core/ but serves only notion-adapter.ts in src/store/ — a layer inversion where a utility is hosted above its sole consumer. Moving notion-map.ts to src/store/ (alongside its consumer) would correct both the physical misplacement and the test directory split, and would reduce the core zone's file count by one entry.
- [call graph] 60 internal calls, 118 outgoing, 1 incoming (cohesion: 0.34, coupling: 0.66)

</insights>
